skill.name: contracts
skill.description: Guides teams through software contract design, materialization, implementation, verification, and change resolution.
skill.summary: Define and manage software interface contracts across parallel development tasks.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found markdown code fences, inline literals, and interface examples rather than executable code or cryptographic APIs. No prompt injection, network access, secret handling, or malicious intent was found. The main residual risk is bounded filesystem writing to memory/contracts files, so publication is acceptable with normal workspace controls.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown fences and inline examples. I found no executable script, shell command, Ruby backtick call, or command injection path in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged areas are documentation blocks with JSON or process descriptions. There is no interpreter directive, executable file, command string, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography locations do not contain MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic operations. They contain lifecycle text and contract examples, so no cryptographic weakness is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no crypto API usage or weak algorithm names at the reported locations. The matches appear to be analyzer noise from ordinary markdown and contract terminology.

low_findings.title: Bounded Filesystem Write Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill allows Write and instructs agents to create or update contract files under memory/contracts. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but hosts should keep normal workspace boundaries in place.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is explicitly documented and limited to contract artifacts. I found no evidence of arbitrary path construction, destructive writes, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Contract Discovery Is Not System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The Glob usage is limited to memory/contracts/*.json for finding project contract files. I found no evidence of host enumeration, sensitive directory scanning, or collection of system metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is a narrow project-local glob used for the documented contract workflow. It does not instruct broad filesystem traversal or reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Parallel development often fails when teams make different assumptions about shared interfaces. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code define, store, verify, and revise explicit software contracts.
actual_capabilities: Defines contracts for cross-task dependencies before implementation starts.
Documents provider and consumer roles for each contract.
Stores contract definitions in memory/contracts JSON files.
Guides implementers to report which contracts they provided or consumed.
Explains how verifiers compare implementations against contract requirements.
Provides a process for resolving contract changes and breaking changes.
limitations: It does not validate contract files with an automated parser.
It does not generate complete application code by itself.
It depends on the host agent to keep file writes inside the workspace.
It is designed for software task coordination, not legal agreements.

use_cases.title: Coordinate parallel feature work
use_cases.description: Define shared interfaces before separate agents build providers and consumers.

use_cases.title: Verify implementation boundaries
use_cases.description: Check that code uses only the methods, data fields, and responses in the contract.

use_cases.title: Manage contract changes
use_cases.description: Assess requested interface changes, mark breaking changes, and reassign affected tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple interface contract
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the contracts skill to define a minimal interface contract for this feature. Include the provider task, consumer tasks, method names, input types, output types, and error cases.

prompt_templates.title: Materialize planned contracts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the contracts skill to turn the design contracts into memory/contracts files. Keep each contract compact, versioned, and clear about provider and consumer roles.

prompt_templates.title: Verify contract compliance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the contracts skill to compare the implementation against the contract. Report missing methods, extra assumptions, type mismatches, and any consumer usage outside the contract.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve a breaking contract change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the contracts skill to evaluate this requested contract change. Decide whether to modify, extend, or reject it, then identify all affected consumers and verification steps.

output_examples.input: A design includes a user repository used by three implementation tasks.
output_examples.output: A concise contract plan with one provider, two consumers, required repository methods, version history, and a storage path under memory/contracts.

output_examples.input: An implementer says an API contract is missing a validation error case.
output_examples.output: A resolution note that assesses impact, recommends extending the contract, increments the version, and lists consumers that need re-verification.

output_examples.input: A verifier reviews code that adds fields not present in the data contract.
output_examples.output: A compliance report that flags the extra fields, explains the contract mismatch, and recommends either implementation changes or contract revision.

best_practices: Create contracts before assigning parallel implementation tasks.
Keep each contract minimal, versioned, and explicit about provider and consumer roles.
Re-verify all consumers after any breaking contract change.
anti_patterns: Do not let implementers invent shared interfaces after coding starts.
Do not change a contract without incrementing the version and recording history.
Do not use this skill for legal, procurement, or employment contracts.

faq.question: Is this skill for legal contracts?
faq.answer: No. It manages software interface contracts between development tasks.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it run external commands?
faq.answer: No evidence of external command execution was found in SKILL.md.

faq.question: Where are contract files stored?
faq.answer: The skill directs agents to use memory/contracts with one JSON file per contract.

faq.question: How does it handle breaking changes?
faq.answer: It requires version increments, history entries, impact assessment, and re-verification of consumers.

faq.question: Can it enforce contracts automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow for agents and reviewers, but it does not include an automated validator.
